dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7975 IH: Feeding Families Not Fear Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Brown (for herself and Mr. McGovern) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Agriculture, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To amend Public Law 119–21 to rescind the increased appropriations for immigration and customs enforcement (ICE), and to reverse the harmful reduction in supplemental nutrition assistance program benefits under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Feeding Families Not Fear Act of 2026.

subsection.header: Repeal of increased appropriations for immigration and customs enforcement
subsection.text: Sections 90003 and 100052 of Public Law 119–21 (139 Stat. 358, 387) (commonly known as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act) are repealed, and the unobligated balances of amounts made available under such sections (as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of this Act) are hereby rescinded.

subsection.header: Restoration of supplemental nutrition assistance program benefits under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008
subsection.text: Subtitle A of title I of Public Law 119–21 (139 Stat. 80–85) is repealed, and all provisions of law amended by such subtitle are restored and revived as if such subtitle had not been enacted into law.
